package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "os"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for stmt_without_close: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Prepared statements or similar DB handles without an observed Close() call.
type CaseStmtWithoutCloseDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseStmtWithoutCloseModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseStmtWithoutCloseRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseStmtWithoutCloseModel
}

type CaseStmtWithoutCloseService struct {
    repo *CaseStmtWithoutCloseRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseStmtWithoutCloseAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveStmtWithoutClose(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseStmtWithoutCloseDTO) error {
    focus := "stmt_without_close"
    _ = focus
    stmt, _ := sqlDB.PrepareContext(ctx, "SELECT id FROM accounts WHERE status = ?")
    _ = stmt
    stmt_close_positive := map[string]int{"stmt": 1, "close": 2}
    stmt_close_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    stmt_positive_0 := stmt_close_positive["stmt"] + len(input)
    _ = stmt_positive_0
    close_positive_1 := stmt_close_positive["close"] + len(input)
    _ = close_positive_1
    return nil
}
